1. Built benchmark livestreaming cases and established scalable methodologies. The share of livestream GMV from T100 Project sellers increased to 20% of the total. In June, there were over 80 sessions exceeding $100K each, including a $2M MEGA LIVE for PopMart Labubu and a $680K new product launch for Oakcha perfume. Meanwhile, livestream GMV accounted for over 50% of total shop GMV for sellers such as Noely and Divi. We consolidated and documented operational methodologies across different industries and provided training to sellers.
2. Identified and drove optimization of several fundamental issues within the livestream ecosystem, including: 1) refining livestream product workflows, improving advertising, GNE, and Pontos-related issues; 2) enhanced user interaction tools; 3) iterated traffic analytics tools such as the LIVE Dashboard, traffic analysis and video-to-live traffic diagnosis; 4) improved hosts’ data analysis and post-analysis action capabilities; 5) collaborated with the Shop Tab team to build the Livestream "Trending" channel and launched the strategy to display livestream cards by default in comprehensive search to strengthen the livestream awareness.
3. Acted as a key driver for the 0-to-1 development of the US livestream ecosystem, we worked with the recommendation & algo team to increase the weighting of content interaction metrics. Previously, the content distribution relied heavily on transaction indicators, leading to an ecosystem dominated by single hero products. After the recommendation algorithm adjustment, the strengthened focus on content-driven livestreams resulted in effective growth in show PV and GPM. The operations team implemented training rapidly, resulting in significant improvements in livestream content quality. The ecosystem is now forming a virtuous cycle, advancing both content strength and transactional performance, establishing a solid base for mid-term to long-term development.

The project involved over ten teams across various departments. Through tight cross-functional collaboration, we overcame coordination challenges and implemented effective solutions. By H1, we upgraded the seller product flow , optimized the free shipping traffic, and integrated with the campaign mechanism to encourage more sellers to provide freeshipping. Together with 2 round withdrawals of shipping subsidy completed and welldone GTM, and the project was delivered a month ahead of schedule. 
After the withdrawal, the free shipping subsidy, the free shipping rate is not only stable but keep increasing(target completion by 116%). The seller self-configured free shippingrate rose from 40% to 75%, saving the project over $100 million in the annual budget."

- **Strategic Impact: **The subscription feature represents critical strategic value for TikTok Shop ecosystem. It provides competitive parity with core rivals Amazon and DTC platforms, enabling enhanced long-term user retention and stable repeat purchases that drive merchant revenue and profitability. Users demonstrate 40% repeat purchase rates in subsequent months. Long term category revenue growth potential is high - Amazon + other DTC sellers see 30% to 60% of their revenue coming from subscriptions.

Dropshipping identification rules and business model risks were clearly defined, with alignment across Recommendation, Search, GNE, and ISV. A full-lifecycle governance framework covering onboarding, publishing, traffic, and fulfillment was launched, driving iterations of the US onboarding, deposit, and ISV mechanisms. The solution was replicated across US FS, the UK, Japan, and Mexico.
In Q4, dropshipping scale significantly contracted, improving marketplace structure and resolving traffic misallocation.
New product volume from dropshipping merchants declined by 90%, and controllable cold-start traffic dropped by 98%. Dropshipping share across supply, traffic, and GMV fell by over 70% (merchant -73%, GMV -77%).
Marketplace efficiency improved, with a 1% statistically significant uplift in 30-day 10-order conversion. Traffic allocation for new products was optimized (POP 60%→50%, L2L 29%→35%, FS 11%→15%). Assortment diversity improved, with per-user transaction clusters +0.24%, and US NPS on product variety +1pt in Q4.

Marketplace efficiency improved, with a 1% statistically significant uplift in 30-day 10-order conversion. Traffic allocation for new products was optimized (POP 60%→50%, L2L 29%→35%, FS 11%→15%). Assortment diversity improved, with per-user transaction clusters +0.24%, and US NPS on product variety +1pt in Q4.

Dropshipping identification rules and business model risks were clearly defined, with alignment across Recommendation, Search, GNE, and ISV. A full-lifecycle governance framework covering onboarding, publishing, traffic, and fulfillment was launched, driving iterations of the US onboarding, deposit, and ISV mechanisms. The solution was replicated across US FS, the UK, Japan, and Mexico.

Premium Offers shoptab focuses on high-end branded products to strengthen user perception of TTS as a destination for good products.
Since launch in October, daily GMV increased 20 times within two months. During BFCM, daily GMV +172% vs. +106pp overall, contributing 11.3% incremental GMV. 37 of 178 products ranked within top 100/1,000 GMV. Penetration among high-LTI users and core female segments reached 73% and 50% (vs. 63% and 41% overall). AB testing showed DAU revisit rate +1% and purchase days per user +0.71%, indicating improved conversion and retention.
To expand supply, the team scaled invitations from 2K to 7K merchants during BFCM, accelerating onboarding and reaching 3K registered products with daily GMV of 110% vs target. Inventory and price monitoring, along with automated allocation tools, improved replenishment efficiency and maintained high-price rate at ~4% (vs. 21% overall).
Through cross-channel coordination—prime exposure, ads, CRM, recommendation traffic, and creator matching—the channel drove strong GMV growth: product cards +303%, short video +125%, live +230%, all outperforming overall levels.

Cross-functional penalty data has been integrated into Thunder, enabling global content governance and business operations teams to track enforcement records. A corresponding monitoring dashboard will be completed by end of 2026 Q1.
Leakage and false positive rates across governance teams were closely monitored and reduced. In collaboration with TNS, mid-risk “weight loss & muscle gain” control rules were removed, and reviewers were retrained for other high false-positive policies. For TikTok Live, precision checks were completed on two high-frequency policies; the recorded-stream model was decommissioned, and leakage cases were fed back to the e-commerce algorithm team for optimization.
To support BFCM, a cross-functional protection mechanism was established. In partnership with the rapid response team, a 7×24 on-call model was launched for Trust & Safety penalties, preventing unexpected live-stream disruptions. Full time-zone coverage and peak-hour on-site support were ensured throughout the campaign.

Cross-functional penalty data has been integrated into Thunder, enabling global content governance and business operations teams to track enforcement records. A corresponding monitoring dashboard will be completed by end of 2026 Q1.
Leakage and false positive rates across governance teams were closely monitored and reduced. In collaboration with TNS, mid-risk “weight loss & muscle gain” control rules were removed, and reviewers were retrained for other high false-positive policies. For TikTok Live, precision checks were completed on two high-frequency policies; the recorded-stream model was decommissioned, and leakage cases were fed back to the e-commerce algorithm team for optimization.
To support BFCM, a cross-functional protection mechanism was established. In partnership with the rapid response team, a 7×24 on-call model was launched for Trust & Safety penalties, preventing unexpected live-stream disruptions. Full time-zone coverage and peak-hour on-site support were ensured throughout the campaign.

**Results**
 In 2025, the US CBT team built and scaled the network from 0 to 1 within three months, covering the West Coast, East Coast, and Texas, and successfully supported the BFCM peak. Daily volume exceeded 220K within three months of launch, with a peak of 470K and stable penetration at 13–14%. Off-peak average delivery time improved from 140+ hours to ~100 hours. The outbound discrepancy rate improved from 0.4% to 0.05%, with stable performance maintained during Black Friday.
**Key Actions**
 The network was built within four months with multiple carriers onboarded, and system and operational transitions were completed within 20 days. A standardized 6-week launch SOP and weekly governance cadence were established, with strengthened process controls, exception monitoring, and KPI integration into contracts to enhance service quality.
**Impact**
 The project enabled scaled fulfillment within a short cycle and established a scalable, replicable, and governable logistics system, laying a strong foundation for the long-term development of a platform-controlled logistics model.
